analysis.core_story_summary: Sage Mankanak was the Manasputra of Kashyap. He had seven sons, who were famously known as Marud Ganas. They are the one who hold all the movable and immovable objects of this world.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **SAGE MANKANAK EULOGIZES SHIVA — Kids (8-12):** Sage Mankanak was the Manasputra of Kashyap. He had seven sons, who were famously known as Marud Ganas. They are the one who hold all the movable and immovable objects of this world. This story teaches dedication and sacrifice, divine grace. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **SAGE MANKANAK EULOGIZES SHIVA — Teens (13-17):** Sage Mankanak was the Manasputra of Kashyap. He had seven sons, who were famously known as Marud Ganas. They are the one who hold all the movable and immovable objects of this world. For teens, the key idea is dedication and sacrifice, divine grace. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **SAGE MANKANAK EULOGIZES SHIVA — Young Adults:** Sage Mankanak was the Manasputra of Kashyap. He had seven sons, who were famously known as Marud Ganas. They are the one who hold all the movable and immovable objects of this world. A modern takeaway is dedication and sacrifice, divine grace. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **SAGE MANKANAK EULOGIZES SHIVA — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** Sage Mankanak was the Manasputra of Kashyap. He had seven sons, who were famously known as Marud Ganas. They are the one who hold all the movable and immovable objects of this world. The episode highlights dedication and sacrifice, divine grace. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **SAGE MANKANAK EULOGIZES SHIVA — Global General Audience:** Sage Mankanak was the Manasputra of Kashyap. He had seven sons, who were famously known as Marud Ganas. They are the one who hold all the movable and immovable objects of this world. In universal terms, it explores dedication and sacrifice, divine grace. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
